# Driver Shift Management

> Manage driver availability through online/offline status toggling, controlling whether a driver appears as available for order dispatch and location tracking.

**Category:** Workflow · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** driver · shift · availability · online-offline · dispatch-eligibility

## What this does

Manage driver availability through online/offline status toggling, controlling whether a driver appears as available for order dispatch and location tracking.

Specifies 4 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **driver_id** *(text, required)* — Identifier of the driver.
- **online** *(boolean, required)* — Whether the driver is currently available for orders.
- **status** *(select, optional)* — Driver operational status.
- **current_job_id** *(text, optional)* — Reference to the active order if the driver is currently on a job.
- **city** *(text, optional)* — City the driver is currently operating in (auto-resolved from location).
- **country** *(text, optional)* — Country the driver is currently in (auto-resolved from location).

## What must be true

- **rule_01:** Only drivers with online=true are eligible to receive adhoc order pings.
- **rule_02:** A driver going offline while on an active order retains the current job until it is completed or reassigned.
- **rule_03:** Driver online status is tracked as a boolean field on the driver record.
- **rule_04:** The platform filters for online drivers when computing nearby available drivers for adhoc dispatch.
- **rule_05:** A driver's city and country are automatically updated from reverse geocoding during location updates.
- **rule_06:** Driver status (active, inactive, on_break) provides finer-grained availability beyond the online boolean.

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Driver Goes Online** — when driver updates their status to online via the app, then Driver appears as available in dispatch queries and begins receiving order pings.
- **Driver Goes Offline** — when driver updates their status to offline via the app, then Driver is no longer dispatched new orders and disappears from active driver lists.
- **Driver Available For Dispatch** — when platform queries for available drivers near a pickup location; driver is online; driver has no active current_job, then Driver is a candidate for order assignment or adhoc ping.
- **Driver Unavailable For Dispatch** — when driver is offline OR has an active current_job, then Driver is excluded from dispatch queries.

## Errors it can return

- `DRIVER_NOT_FOUND` — Driver not found.
- `DRIVER_OFFLINE` — This driver is currently offline.

## Connects to

- **driver-assignment-dispatch** *(required)* — Dispatch eligibility depends on driver online status.
- **driver-location-streaming** *(recommended)* — Location updates typically only flow when driver is online.
- **driver-app-flow** *(required)* — Driver app controls the online/offline toggle.
